name: María de Huerva and Collado Sillón Loop
name_original: Castillo de María de Huerva - Balsa Corralé - Collado Sillón — circular desde María de Huerva
description: This difficult 13.7-mile hike near María de Huerva explores a historic castle, a serene lake, and a mountain pass with 1341 feet of gain.
region_intro: Hiking around María De Huerva offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, dry riverbeds, and open plains. The region features a mix of agricultural land and more rugged, natural areas, providing varied terrain for outdoor exploration. Trails often lead through barrancos (ravines) and offer views of the surrounding countryside. The area provides opportunities for both leisurely walks and more challenging long distance trails near María De Huerva. Best hiking trails in María De Huerva The most popular hiking route is Barranco de las Almunias Circular Trail, a 11.5 miles (18.6 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours 11 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain through a notable ravine. Another top favourite among local hikers is María de Huerva and Collado Sillón Loop, a difficult 13.7 miles (22.1 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain, including open areas and ascents to viewpoints like Collado Sillón. Local hikers also love the Mirador de las Planas – Castillo de Cadrete loop from Cadrete, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail leading through hilly landscapes with historical points of interest, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Hiking around María De Huerva is defined by rolling hills, barrancos, and open landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy hikes María De Huerva to challenging long distance trails. The routes in María De Huerva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore María De Huerva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around María De Huerva?
A: There are over 40 hiking trails in the María De Huerva region, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from easy strolls to more challenging long-distance routes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in María De Huerva?
A: Yes, María De Huerva offers several easy trails. For a pleasant, shorter walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31912327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Cadrete – View of Castillo de Cadrete loop from Cadrete&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.6 km long and features gentle elevation changes, perfect for a relaxed outing.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in María De Huerva?
A: Many of the trails in María De Huerva are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point without retracing your steps. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974886865/barranco-de-las-almunias-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de las Almunias Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14948103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Planas – Castillo de Cadrete loop from Cadrete&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking in the area?
A: The region boasts several interesting natural and historical landmarks. You can explore viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/94970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Planas&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6575360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de las Zorras&lt;/a&gt;, or discover unique natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muel Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojo de las Almunias&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging long-distance trails around María De Huerva?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974956789/maria-de-huerva-and-collado-sillon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;María de Huerva and Collado Sillón Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 22.1 km path that traverses varied terrain and offers significant ascents to viewpoints.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in María De Huerva?
A: The trails in María De Huerva are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.1 stars from over 25 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from rolling hills and barrancos to open plains, and the well-maintained paths.

Q: Are the hiking trails in María De Huerva suitable for dogs?
A: Many trails in the María De Huerva region are dog-friendly, offering open spaces and varied terrain for you and your canine companion to enjoy. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially near agricultural areas or wildlife, and to carry enough water for both of you.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in María De Huerva?
A: The best seasons for hiking in María De Huerva are typically spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience, though some paths might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by historical sites or castles?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical points of interest. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14948103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Planas – Castillo de Cadrete loop from Cadrete&lt;/a&gt; leads through hilly landscapes and past the historic Castillo de Cadrete, offering a blend of nature and history.

Q: Are there options for moderate hikes in María De Huerva?
A: Absolutely. The region has 26 moderate trails. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5330339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;María de Huerva Castle – Collado Sillón Cairn loop from María de Huerva&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 15.1 km route with moderate elevation changes, providing a rewarding experience without being overly strenuous.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the varied terrain of María De Huerva includes several elevated points offering panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/94970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Planas&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6575360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de las Zorras&lt;/a&gt; are notable viewpoints where you can enjoy expansive vistas of the surrounding countryside and rolling hills.
tips: The fortress housed the troops of Abderramán III in the year 935 and served in the defense of Zaragoza against Alfonso I the battler in 1118. After the Christian conquest, the castle passed into the hands of the kings of Aragon, and had numerous owners.

The Balsa de Corralé in María de Huerva is a charming place to enjoy nature. It's a water reservoir, ideal for peaceful walks and birdwatching.
A hiking trail of approximately 7 kilometers runs through the Morera ravine and the Balsa Trail.

The Collado del Sillón in María de Huerva is a very interesting hiking trail that offers spectacular views and an enriching experience. From the summit, you can enjoy panoramic views of Moncayo and the Pyrenees.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Castillo de María de Huerva - Balsa Corralé - Collado Sillón circular trail?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult and requires a very good fitness level. It covers approximately 22.1 kilometers with an elevation gain of about 410 meters. While most paths are accessible, some sections demand firm footing, so appropriate footwear is essential.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable spots. The historic María de Huerva Castle (La Atalaya), a 10th-century Muslim-origin monument, offers a glimpse into the region's past. The serene Balsa de Corralé is a picturesque water reservoir perfect for tranquil moments and birdwatching. You'll also pass by the Collado Sillón Cairn and traverse natural landscapes like the Morera Gorge.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of accessible paths, but be prepared for varied ground. Given the elevation changes and the presence of a 'collado' (mountain pass), you can expect a mix of wider tracks and narrower, potentially rockier paths in steeper areas. Firm footing is essential in some sections.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: For hiking, it's estimated to take approximately 6 hours and 8 minutes to complete the full 22.1-kilometer loop. If you're running, it could take around 2 hours and 42 minutes.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo de María de Huerva - Balsa Corralé - Collado Sillón trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially around wildlife or other hikers, and be mindful of local regulations regarding leashes. Remember to carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: While specific recommendations aren't provided, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions for hiking in this region, with milder temperatures. Summer can be very hot, making early morning starts advisable, and winter might bring colder temperatures, though snow is less common at lower elevations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access the Castillo de María de Huerva - Balsa Corralé - Collado Sillón circular trail.

faqs.question: Where can I park for this trail, and is public transport an option?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations aren't detailed, you would typically look for parking in or around María de Huerva, which serves as the starting point for this circular route. For public transport, you would need to check local bus services to María de Huerva, as direct trailhead access via public transport is not specified.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through sections of several other paths, including Barranco de la Morera, La Vallovera, Arista de Enmedio, Variante Nisupu, and Conector Arista Sillon-Enmedio, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring or wear for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, it's crucial to wear appropriate hiking footwear with good grip. Bring plenty of water, especially on warmer days, and snacks. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is recommended, and layers of clothing are always a good idea to adapt to changing weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any easier alternatives nearby for families or less experienced hikers?
faqs.answer: While this specific trail is difficult, the area around María de Huerva likely offers easier, shorter routes. For a less demanding experience, you might consider exploring just the immediate vicinity of the Balsa de Corralé for a tranquil stroll, or look for other local trails specifically marked as easy or family-friendly.
